title: Enum Handling Pattern — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: **AI Summary**: Frontend enum lifecycle for Angular components consuming `EnumDTO` from the API. Covers three concerns: (1) dropdown binding uses `EnumDTO` directly from the API — no local constants needed, (2) programmatic comparison uses `as const` objects with string values matching C# enum member names, compared against `EnumDTO.name`, (3) grid rendering uses `EnumDTO.name` for display text and constants for conditional styling. Local constants are only created for enums that code switche...

</select>

core/models/
├── enum-dto.ts                 # EnumDTO interface
├── named-dto.ts                # NamedDTO interface
├── grid-list-response.ts       # GridListResponse<T> interface
└── shared.enums.ts             # Cross-feature enum constants (if any)

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: - [ADR-0004](../../adr/0004-enum-over-string-constants-for-status-fields.md) - Backend uses C# enums for system-owned finite status, kind, and mode fields only.
- [ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md) - API transports true enums as EnumDTO; FK/catalogue references use NamedDTO or explicit stable Code fields.

- [ADR-0028](../../adr/0028-require-owning-admin-modules-for-user-configurable-configuration.md) - Tenant-configurable lookup values require owning admin modules and must not be modeled as local enums.
- [ADR-0023](../../adr/0023-as-const-enum-constants-on-web.md) - Frontend uses as const objects, not TypeScript enum, for system enum comparison constants.
- [Feature Service](feature-service.md) - Model definitions, EnumDTO/NamedDTO shared types, dropdown binding roundtrip.

sections.title: The Core Principle
content: > The API is the single source of truth for system enum display labels and dropdown options. The owning admin module is the single source of truth for tenant-configurable catalogue values.
Three tiers of frontend enum handling - use the simplest tier that fits:
| Tier | When | What to Define | Example |
| Display only | True enum appears in grid columns, badges, read-only fields | Nothing - use EnumDTO.name from the DTO | Grid status column |
| Dropdown binding | User selects a true enum value in a form | Nothing - use EnumDTO[] from lookup endpoint | Lifecycle status dropdown |
| Programmatic comparison | Code branches on a true enum value | as const object in {entity}.enums.ts | If status is Active, show green badge |

For tenant-configurable values such as Action Types or RCA Methods, do not use this enum pattern. Load options from the owning admin module lookup endpoint and bind NamedDTO, stable Code, or the module-specific lookup DTO required by that contract.

sections.title: Tier 1: Display Only
content: When an enum value is simply shown to the user (grid column, detail field), use `EnumDTO.name` directly. No local constants needed.

sections.title: Tier 2: True Enum Dropdown Binding
content: Dropdowns for true system enums are populated from lookup endpoints that return EnumDTO[]. The selected value roundtrips to the API without transformation ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)).
Tenant-configurable catalogue dropdowns are different: load them from their owning admin module /lookups endpoint and bind the contract shape that module exposes, typically NamedDTO plus stable code.

sections.title: Tier 3: Programmatic Comparison
content: When component logic branches on an enum value, define an `as const` object with string values matching the C# enum member names. Compare against `EnumDTO.name`. See [ADR-0023](../../adr/0023-as-const-enum-constants-on-web.md).
Create `{entity}.enums.ts` colocated with the feature models:
export type ActionStatus = (typeof ActionStatus)[keyof typeof ActionStatus];
export type EvidenceType = (typeof EvidenceType)[keyof typeof EvidenceType];
- Values are **string names** matching C# enum member names (`'Active'`, not `0`)
- No display labels — those come from `EnumDTO.name` on the DTO
- Only define constants for enums you actually compare against in code
- File lives alongside `{entity}.models.ts` in the feature folder
- Shared constants (used across features) go in `core/models/`
Combining Tier 1 (display via `name`) with Tier 3 (conditional class via constant):

sections.title: What NOT to Do
content: // ✗ Don't use TypeScript enum — broken with --isolatedModules, non-erasable
// ✗ Don't use const enum — broken with Angular CLI's --isolatedModules
// ✗ Don't duplicate display labels — server is the source of truth
// ✗ Don't use enum types as DTO fields — breaks dropdown binding (ADR-0005)
// ✗ Don't use magic strings for comparison — use named constants
// ✗ Don't define constants for enums you never compare against
// If evidenceType only appears in dropdowns, skip it

sections.title: File Structure
content: ├── action.enums.ts        # as const objects (only for comparison)
Shared enums used across multiple features:

sections.title: Relationship to Other Patterns
content: | [Feature Service](feature-service.md) | EnumDTO/NamedDTO type definition, model rules, dropdown roundtrip, lookup methods | True enum binding rules and comparison constants |
| [Configuration Ownership and E2E Data](configuration-ownership-e2e-data.md) | Admin ownership for configurable lookup data | Boundary between true enums and tenant catalogues |
| [Capture Page](capture-page.md) | Form layout, save flow | Dropdown binding with API-provided options |
| [List Page](list-page.md) | Grid setup, toolbar | Grid column rendering with EnumDTO.name for true enums and lookup display fields for catalogues |
| [Validation](validation.md) | Per-field errors, duplicate detection | Backend validation for enum ordinals and catalogue ids/codes |

sections.title: See Also
content: - [ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md) — EnumDTO/NamedDTO API transport and zero-transformation roundtrip
- [ADR-0023](../../adr/0023-as-const-enum-constants-on-web.md) — Why `as const` objects, not TypeScript `enum`; why string values, not numeric IDs
